name: Raglan 500
description: A varied Waikato loop with coastal sections, gravel roads, and steady climbing.
difficultyExplanation: You will handle mixed gravel roads, coastal wind, and steady climbing over multiple days. Resupply exists, but the gaps are long enough that some planning is still required.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: Exposed coastal and ridgeline segments can become significantly slower in strong wind, making pacing and resupply timing more important than expected.
routeSummary: Raglan 500 is a intermediate bikepacking route covering about 498 km, commonly ridden over 3 days. A varied Waikato loop with coastal sections, gravel roads, and steady climbing. Riders can expect 55% Gravel / 25% Road / 20% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Year-round, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: Exposed coastal and ridgeline segments can become significantly slower in strong wind, making pacing and resupply timing more important than expected. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 3-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
